title: File Downloads Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of file downloads and secure access in modern web applications.

questions.question: Which of these Express.js methods is specifically designed for serving a file for download with the correct headers?
options: res.send()
res.json()
res.download()
res.redirect()

questions.question: What's the primary benefit of the 'res.download()' helper in Express?
options: It automatically encrypts the file before sending.
It sets the correct 'Content-Disposition' header so the user's browser prompts them to save the file to disk.
It automatically generates a thumbail for each image.
It's the only way to store files in a database.

questions.question: Why is it generally a good security practice NOT to use sequential IDs for your file URLs (e.g., /download/1, /download/2)?
options: It makes the database slower.
It allows an attacker to guess the ID of other users' files and potentially download them (IDOR attack).
It's not supported by modern browsers.
It's a requirement of all CSS frameworks.

questions.question: What is the 'Content-Disposition' header useful for?
options: To encrypt files using a secret key.
To instruct the browser whether to display the file inline in the browser tab or as an attachment to be downloaded.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: How can you offload the bandwidth cost and server load of serving large files if you're using cloud storage like S3?
options: By automatically resizing images for different devices.
By redirecting the user's browser directly to a Pre-signed URL generated from S3.
By only using a CDN for serving assets.
None of the above.
